article_title: Gothic 1 Remake Launches With Divergent Reviews: Critics Praise the World, Fault the Combat and 30 FPS Console Performance

content_preview.title: Gothic 1 Remake Launches With Divergent Reviews: Critics Praise the World, Fault the Combat and 30 FPS Console Performance
content_preview.summary: Alkimia Interactive and THQ Nordic's remake of the 2001 RPG launched June 5 to a 73 Metacritic on PC and 66 on PS5, with reviewers split between its atmosphere and its rough combat and console frame rates.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Gothic 1 Remake, a modern reconstruction of Piranha Bytes' 2001 action role-playing game, launched on June 5, 2026 for PlayStation 5, Windows, and Xbox Series X/S, according to [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Gothic_1_Remake). The game was developed by Alkimia Interactive and published by THQ Nordic, as [GameWatcher](https://www.gamewatcher.com/gothic-1-remake-release-date) also reports. Built in Unreal Engine 5, the remake preserves the original premise of an unnamed pris...

editor_notes.content_quality: Thorough, well-balanced launch/review-roundup News piece (974 words) that cleanly separates the split critical reception (atmosphere vs. combat/performance) and attributes each score and quote to its single outlet.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 7 snapshots read from disk (HTTP 200). source-0 (Wikipedia) confirms the '5 June 2026' release, the PS5/Windows/Xbox platforms, and the studio history (Piranha Bytes acquired May 2019, remake announced February 2020, Alkimia founded March 2021, the December 2019 Gothic Playable Teaser, the 'complete reboot', the UE5 switch, ELEX). source-2 (Metacritic) confirms 'PC Based on 9 Critic Reviews 73', 'PlayStation 5 Based on 4 Critic Reviews 66', and the tbd Xbox score. source-1 (GameWatcher) confirms the £41.99 / £169.99 prices and the Collector's Edition contents. source-6 (Green Man Gaming) confirms the 600+ faces, ~20% larger map, ~15 hours of new content, and the lock-on rework. source-4 (AltChar) confirms the 7/10 score and the 'survival and discovery', 'face models and environments … best in the genre', 'combat is clunky and slow, with no lock-on option', and '~25 FPS' quotes (Asmir Kovacevic). source-5 (Space4Games) confirms the 2.5/5 score and the 'excellent voice acting and a fitting soundtrack', 'atmospherically strong remake … uncompromising direction', 'sluggish, unreliable, imprecise, and frustrating', and 'hardly any guidance, not even optionally' quotes (Josef Erl). source-3 (PlayStation Universe) confirms the '30 FPS on PS5 and PS5 Pro', 'no performance mode', 'unapologetically holding on to its roots', 'two to three hits', and the review headline.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every score, price, count, date, and quote is verbatim in the cited outlet (the apparent mismatches were title-case pros/cons bullets the article correctly lowercased mid-sentence). The cross-outlet FPS discrepancy (PSU's 30 FPS vs AltChar's ~25 FPS) is attributed to each outlet separately — no compound citation conflates them.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional; 'What We Don't Know' appropriately notes the still-forming aggregate, the absent user score, and the unannounced patch status.
editor_notes.originality: New topic — distinct from the just-covered CD Projekt Red Witcher 3 story.
recommendations: Added metacritic.com, gamewatcher.com, psu.com, altchar.com, space4games.com, and greenmangaming.com to the allowlist (the standard review aggregator and established gaming outlets).
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality, fully-verified gaming submission with disciplined single-outlet score/quote attribution. APPROVE.
